event: Saint Agatha with Palm and Platter
symbols: palm branch — the martyr's victory over death and persecution
platter — the instrument of Agatha's specific martyrdom, traditionally holding her severed breasts
golden halo — heavenly sanctity and glory
downcast eyes and serene expression — the peace that surpasses understanding, maintained through suffering
description: A half-length portrait of a young woman with gentle, downcast eyes and a serene expression. She wears a golden-yellow dress with a gathered neckline and a purple-brown mantle draped over one shoulder. A golden halo encircles her head. In her right hand she holds a tall palm branch, and in her left she holds what appears to be a shallow platter or dish. The background is a blue sky with clouds. Her hair is dark and partially covered by a veil. The painting style is refined and luminous, with soft modeling of the face and careful attention to the textures of the garments.

_meta.title: Agatha Serving Her Severed Breasts on a Platter
